mainHtml:
Home/Solutions/DFM Check

DFM Manufacturability Check

Identify wall thickness, hole depth, chamfer, fixturing, material and post-processing risks before quotation and production so designs can enter manufacturing more easily.

Wall Thickness AnalysisHole Depth ReviewTolerance SuggestionsPre-Quote Feedback
Upload Model

Check Content

DFM results support manufacturing decisions. The final machining plan still depends on drawings, materials, equipment and engineering confirmation.

Structural Risk

  • Minimum wall thickness, deep cavities, sharp corners and weak areas.
  • Hole depth, tool reachability and fixturing interference.

Cost Risk

  • Complex features, overly tight tolerances and special post-processing.
  • Batch quantity, urgent needs and added inspection items.

Delivery Risk

  • Material availability, finishing cycle and reinspection requirements.
  • Missing files or inconsistent drawing and model information.

Output Flow

01
Upload Models and DrawingsSupport building basic information from model, drawing, material, quantity and use.
02
AI Initial RecognitionIdentify geometric features and obvious risks to form initial manufacturability notes.
03
Engineering ReviewReview key items against equipment, tooling, process and inspection requirements.
04
Feedback SuggestionsOutput modification suggestions, alternative processes and factors that may affect cost or lead time.

How to Use Check Results

DFM results separate design, cost and delivery risks to help users make tradeoffs before redesign, quotation and ordering.

Design RevisionReduce machining difficultyFeed thin walls, deep holes, sharp corners, interference and tool-inaccessible areas back to design early.
Quotation JudgmentClarify cost sourcesShow how tight tolerances, special materials, complex fixturing and finishing requirements affect price.
Delivery PreparationExpose risks earlyIdentify how material cycle, inspection needs, reinspection and missing documents affect lead time.

Check First, Then Quote

Use DFM to clarify design, cost and manufacturing risks early.
